checks.name: schema_completeness
checks.detail: All required payload fields present and substantive

checks.name: flow_integrity
checks.detail: Use case flows present with connected nodes, valid edges, and error handling nodes

checks.name: trigger_validity
checks.detail: Schedule triggers with valid cron expressions for weekly audit cadence

checks.name: connector_role_alignment
checks.detail: hubspot=crm, notion=knowledge_base, slack=chat_messaging — valid roles (hubspot crm role may need to be added to connectorRoles.ts)

checks.name: adoption_question_fitness
checks.detail: 5 adoption_questions added: hubspot_portal_id, notion_database_id, slack_channel, auto_repair_safe, stale_record_days

checks.name: safety_scan
checks.detail: 0 critical findings. Risky changes (merges, deletions) require human approval. Auto-repair is limited to safe operations.

dimensions.name: Prompt Completeness
dimensions.rationale: Identity 120+ words with CRM context. Instructions cover duplicate detection, stale record identification, missing field analysis, formatting standardization, auto-repair vs. flagged-for-review, and quality score tracking. Tool guidance covers HubSpot, Notion, Slack APIs. Examples and error handling present.

dimensions.name: Tool-Prompt Alignment
dimensions.rationale: All tools mapped to specific operations. HubSpot CRM API endpoints documented. Notion for report logging. Slack for notifications. No phantom tools.

dimensions.name: Trigger Coherence
dimensions.rationale: Weekly schedule trigger matches the audit cadence described in the prompt. Could benefit from an additional trigger for on-demand audits.

dimensions.name: Connector Coverage
dimensions.rationale: All 3 service_flow entries fully backed by connector definitions with credential fields and setup instructions.

dimensions.name: Memory Design
dimensions.rationale: Data quality score tracking over time, known duplicate pairs, repair history. Agent memory scope is well-defined for longitudinal quality monitoring.

dimensions.name: Error Handling
dimensions.rationale: Covers HubSpot API rate limits, auth failures, Notion write failures, and Slack notification failures. Safe vs. risky repair distinction is a strong safety pattern.

dimensions.name: Use Case Fidelity
dimensions.rationale: Main audit flows covered: duplicate scanning, stale detection, missing fields, auto-repair, human review flagging, and quality score reporting.

dimensions.name: Value Clarity
dimensions.rationale: Clear outcome: replaces manual data cleanup sprints with automated ongoing vigilance. CRM data quality directly impacts pipeline accuracy and forecasting.

dimensions.name: Execution Feasibility
dimensions.rationale: HubSpot, Notion, Slack APIs are standard. HubSpot CRM API has good documentation. Auto-repair scope is well-bounded to safe operations.

dimensions.name: Differentiation
dimensions.rationale: AI-powered duplicate detection, fix recommendation generation, safe vs. risky repair classification, quality score trending — substantial reasoning that cannot be replicated with simple automation.

dimensions.name: Variable Necessity
dimensions.rationale: All 5 questions target genuine config needs. auto_repair_safe is critical human-in-the-loop control. stale_record_days allows policy customization.

dimensions.name: Default Quality
dimensions.rationale: Good defaults for channel, auto_repair (Yes), stale_record_days (90). hubspot_portal_id and notion_database_id must be user-provided.

issues: No adoption_questions existed — 5 added covering HubSpot, Notion, Slack, auto-repair policy, and stale record threshold
HubSpot connector role 'crm' may need to be added to connectorRoles.ts
HubSpot and Notion connectors may not have builtin definitions

template: Added 5 adoption_questions: hubspot_portal_id, notion_database_id, slack_channel, auto_repair_safe, stale_record_days
Used boolean type for auto_repair_safe with human_in_the_loop category
Used select type with day options for stale_record_days
